data.name: الرَّحْمَنُ
data.description: "Ar-Rahman" means the Being whose mercy and compassion are immensely vast, benefiting everyone.

quran.data:
الرَّحۡمٰنِ الرَّحِیۡمِ

All praise is for Allah—Lord of all worlds,

- Surah Al-Fatihah, Verse: 2

وَاِلٰـہُکُمۡ اِلٰہٌ وَّاحِدٌ ۚ لَاۤ اِلٰہَ اِلَّا ہُوَ الرَّحۡمٰنُ الرَّحِیۡم

Your God is ˹only˺ One God. There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him—the Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 163

تَنۡزِیۡلٌ مِّنَ الرَّحۡمٰنِ الرَّحِیۡمِ

˹This is˺ a revelation from the Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.

- Surah Fussilat, Verse: 2

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful. اَلْمَلِكُ (Al-Malik): The True King and Absolute Sovereign. اَلْقُدُّوْسُ (Al-Quddus): The Most Holy. اَلسَّلَامُ (As-Salam): The One whose essential attribute is Peace. اَلْمُؤْمِنُ (Al-Mu'min): The Giver of Peace and Security. اَلْمُهَيْمِنُ (Al-Muhaymin): The Complete Overseer. اَلْعَزِيْزُ (Al-Aziz): The All-Mighty. اَلْجَبَّارُ (Al-Jabbar): The Compeller, under whose command all creation moves. اَلْمُتَكَبِّرُ (Al-Mutakabbir): The Truly Supreme. اَلْخَالِقُ (Al-Khaliq): The Creator. اَلْبَارِئُ (Al-Bari): The Evolver. اَلْمُصَوِّرُ (Al-Musawwir): The Shaper of Forms, the Skilled Artist. اَلْغَفَّارُ (Al-Ghaffar): The Repeatedly Forgiving. اَلْقَهَّارُ (Al-Qahhar): The Subduer, before whom everyone is helpless and powerless. اَلْوَهَّابُ (Al-Wahhab): The Bestower of abundance without expecting anything in return. اَلرَّزَّاقُ (Ar-Razzaq): The Provider for all. اَلْفَتَّاحُ (Al-Fattah): The Opener of the doors of mercy and sustenance for all. اَلْعَلِیْمُ (Al-Alim): The All-Knowing of everything. اَلْقَابِضُ (Al-Qabid): The Constrictor. اَلْبَاسِطُ (Al-Basit): The Expander, meaning He creates constriction or expansion for anyone according to His wisdom and will. اَلْخَافِضُ (Al-Khafid): The Abaser. اَلرَّافِعُ (Ar-Rafi): The Exalter, meaning He alone raises or lowers whomever He wills, whenever He wills. اَلْمُعِزُّ (Al-Mu'izz): The Giver of Honor. اَلْمُذِلُّ (Al-Mudhill): The Giver of Dishonor; it is within His will to bestow honor or plunge someone into the depths of disgrace. اَلسَّمِيْعُ (As-Sami): The All-Hearing. اَلْبَصِيْرُ (Al-Basir): The All-Seeing. اَلْحَكَمُ (Al-Hakam): The Ultimate Judge. اَلْعَدْلُ (Al-Adl): The Utterly Just. اَللَّطِيْفُ (Al-Latif): The Subtle One, whose essential attribute is grace and kindness. اَلْخَبِيْرُ (Al-Khabir): The All-Aware of every matter. اَلْحَلِيْمُ (Al-Halim): The Most Forbearing. اَلْعَظِيْمُ (Al-Azim): The Magnificent, the Most High. اَلْغَفُوْرُ (Al-Ghafur): The All-Forgiving. اَلشَّكُوْরُ (Ash-Shakur): The Appreciative and the Bestower of excellent rewards for good deeds. اَلْعَلِيُّ (Al-Ali): The Highest. اَلْكَبِيْرُ (Al-Kabir): The Most Great. اَلْحَفِيْظُ (Al-Hafiz): The Preserver of all. اَلْمُقِيْتُ (Al-Muqit): The Nourisher who supplies the means of life to all. اَلْحَسِيْبُ (Al-Hasib): The Sufficient One for all. اَلْجَلِيْلُ (Al-Jalil): The Majestic. اَلْكَرِيْمُ (Al-Karim): The Most Generous. اَلرَّقِيْبُ (Ar-Raqib): The Watchful and Guardian. اَلْمُجِيْبُ (Al-Mujib): The Responsive. اَلوَاسِعُ (Al-Wasi): The All-Encompassing, the Expander. اَلْحَكِيْمُ (Al-Hakim): The All-Wise. اَلْوَدُوْدُ (Al-Wadud): The Most Loving. اَلْمَجِيْدُ (Al-Majid): The All-Glorious. اَلْبَاعِثُ (Al-Ba'ith): The Resurrector—who will raise the dead after death. اَلشَّهِيْدُ (Ash-Shahid): The Witness who observes and hears everything. اَلْحَقُّ (Al-Haqq): The Truth, whose essence and existence are absolute Truth. اَلْوَكِيْلُ (Al-Wakil): The Trustee. اَلْقَوِيُّ (Al-Qawi): The Most Strong. اَلْمَتِيْنُ (Al-Matin): The Firm and Powerful. اَلْوَلِيُّ (Al-Wali): The Protecting Friend and Helper. اَلْحَمِيْدُ (Al-Hamid): The Most Praiseworthy. اَلْمُحْصِيُ (Al-Muhsi): The Counter, who is fully aware and knowledgeable of all creation. اَلْمُبْدِئُ (Al-Mubdi): The Originator who gives existence for the first time. اَلْمُعِيِدُ (Al-Mu'id): The Restorer of life. اَلْمُحْيِي (Al-Muhyi): The Giver of Life. اَلْمُمِيْتُ (Al-Mumit): The Giver of Death. اَلْحَيُّ (Al-Hayy): The Ever-Living. اَلْقَيُّوْمُ (Al-Qayyum): The Self-Subsisting Sustainer of all. اَلْوَاجِدُ (Al-Wajid): The Perceiver of all. اَلْمَاجِدُ (Al-Majid): The Noble and Magnificent. اَلْوَاحِدُ (Al-Wahid): The One. اَلْاَحَدُ (Al-Ahad): The Unique in His attributes. اَلصَّمَدُ (As-Samad): The Eternal, who is not dependent on anyone, while everyone is dependent on Him. اَلْقَدِيْرُ (Al-Qadir): The Able. اَلْمُقْتَدِرُ (Al-Muqtadir): The Powerful and Controller over all. اَلْمُقَدِّمُ (Al-Muqaddim): The Expediter, who brings forward whomever He wills. اَلْمُؤَخِّرُ (Al-Mu'akhkhir): The Delayer, who puts back whomever He wills. اَلأَوَّلُ (Al-Awwal): The First—who existed when no one else did. اَلْآخِرُ (Al-Akhir): The Last—who will remain when no one else exists. اَلظَّاهِرُ (Az-Zahir): The Manifest and Fully Evident. اَلْبَاطِنُ (Al-Batin): The Hidden. اَلْوَالِيْ (Al-Wali): The Governor and Trustee. اَلْمُتَعَالِيْ (Al-Muta'ali): The Most Exalted. اَلْبَرُّ (Al-Barr): The Most Beneficent. اَلتَّوَّাবُ (At-Tawwab): The Accepter of Repentance. اَلْمُنْتَقِمُ (Al-Muntaqim): The Avenger against sinners. اَلْعَفُوُّ (Al-Afuww): The Pardoner and Forgiver. اَلرَّؤُوْفُ (Ar-Ra'uf): The Most Kind. مَالِكُ الْمُلْكِ (Malikul-Mulk): The Owner of Sovereignty of the entire universe. ذُو الْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرَامِ (Dhul-Jalali wal-Ikram): The Lord of Majesty and Generosity. اَلْمُقْسِطُ (Al-Muqsit): The Equitable who grants the rights of the rightful. اَلْجَامِعُ (Al-Jami): The Gatherer of the whole universe on the Day of Judgment. اَلْغَنِيُّ (Al-Ghani): The Self-Sufficient. اَلْمُغْنِيْ (Al-Mughni): The Enricher who makes others independent. اَلْمَانِعُ (Al-Mani): The Preventer of what should be stopped. اَلضَّارُّ (Ad-Darr): The Inflictor of harm. اَلنَّافِعُ (An-Nafi): The Bestower of benefit. النُّوْرُ (An-Nur): The Light. اَلْهَادِي (Al-Hadi): The Provider of Guidance. اَلْبَدِيْعُ (Al-Badi): The Incomparable Creator without any prior model. اَلْبَاقِيْ (Al-Baqi): The Everlasting who will never perish. اَلْوَارِثُ (Al-Warith): The Inheritor who will remain after everything fades away. اَلرَّشِيْدُ (Ar-Rashid): The Guide to the Right Path, whose every action is purposeful. اَلصَّبُوْরُ (As-Sabur): The Most Patient, who does not immediately punish despite witnessing extreme audacity and disobedience. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الرَّحِيمُ
data.description: "Ar-Rahim" means the Being whose mercy is abundant; upon whomever His mercy is bestowed, it is granted in full.

data.name: الْمَلِكُ
data.description: This name expresses Allah's sovereignty, absolute power, and authority. Allah is the Being who holds full power over all His creation, who is the true Sovereign. He gives commands and prohibitions to His servants.

quran.data:
یُسَبِّحُ لِلّٰہِ مَا فِی السَّمٰوٰتِ وَمَا فِی الۡاَرۡضِ الۡمَلِکِ الۡقُدُّوۡسِ الۡعَزِیۡزِ الۡحَکِیۡمِ

Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth ˹constantly˺ glorifies Allah—the King, the Most Holy, the Almighty, the All-Wise.

- Surah Al-Jumuah, Verse: 1

ہُوَ اللّٰہُ الَّذِیۡ لَاۤ اِلٰہَ اِلَّا ہُوَ ۚ اَلۡمَلِکُ الۡقُدُّوۡسُ السَّلٰمُ الۡمُؤۡمِنُ الۡمُہَیۡمِنُ الۡعَزِیۡزُ الۡجَبَّارُ الۡمُتَکَبِّرُ ؕ سُبۡحٰنَ اللّٰہِ عَمَّا یُشۡرِکُوۡنَ

He is Allah—there is no god except Him: the King, the Most Holy, the All-Perfect, the Source of Serenity, the Watcher ˹of all˺, the Almighty, the Supreme in Might, the Majestic. Glorified is Allah far above what they associate with Him ˹in worship˺!

- Surah Al-Hashr, Verse: 23

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful. اَلْمَلِكُ (Al-Malik): The True King and Absolute Sovereign. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْقُدُّوسُ
data.description: "Al-Quddus" is the Being who is exceedingly holy and free from everything considered evil. He is magnificent in every aspect and free from all defects.

data.name: السَّلاَمُ
data.description: "As-Salam" is the Being who grants peace and security to all creation. In a different sense, "As-Salam" refers to the Being who is free and pure from all kinds of defects and imperfections; and this word expresses this concept of purity very emphatically.

data.name: الْمُؤْمِنُ
data.description: "Al-Mu'min" means the Being who provides security to His servants from all fears and dangers.

data.name: الْمُهَيْمِنُ
data.description: "Al-Muhaymin" means the Being who preserves and supervises everything.

data.name: الْعَزِيزُ
data.description: Al-Aziz' means: He is Powerful, Honorable, and Invincible, whom no one can defeat or suppress. His honor and power are supreme; He is unrivaled.

quran.data:
وَتَوَکَّلۡ عَلَی الۡعَزِیۡزِ الرَّحِیۡمِ

Put your trust in the Almighty, Most Merciful,

- Surah Ash-Shu'ara, Verse: 217

ہُوَ اللّٰہُ الَّذِیۡ لَاۤ اِلٰہَ اِلَّا ہُوَ ۚ اَلۡمَلِکُ الۡقُدُّوۡسُ السَّلٰمُ الۡمُؤۡمِنُ الۡمُہَیۡمِنُ الۡعَزِیۡزُ الۡجَبَّارُ الۡمُتَکَبِّرُ ؕ سُبۡحٰنَ اللّٰہِ عَمَّا یُشۡرِکُوۡنَ

˹He is the˺ Knower of the seen and unseen—the Almighty, All-Wise.

- Surah At-Taghabun, Verse: 18

data.name: الْجَبَّارُ
data.description: Al-Jabbar' means: He keeps His creation subject to His will. No one has the opportunity to go beyond His will.

quran.data:
ہُوَ اللّٰہُ الَّذِیۡ لَاۤ اِلٰہَ اِلَّا ہُوَ ۚ اَلۡمَلِکُ الۡقُدُّوۡسُ السَّلٰمُ الۡمُؤۡمِنُ الۡمُہَیۡمِنُ الۡعَزِیۡزُ الۡجَبَّارُ الۡمُتَکَبِّرُ ؕ سُبۡحٰنَ اللّٰہِ عَمَّا یُشۡرِکُوۡنَ

He is Allah—there is no god except Him: the King, the Most Holy, the All-Perfect, the Source of Serenity, the Watcher ˹of all˺, the Almighty, the Supreme in Might, the Majestic. Glorified is Allah far above what they associate with Him ˹in worship˺!

- Surah Al-Hashr, Verse: 23

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُتَكَبِّرُ
data.description: Al-Mutakabbir' – He is the Being who is superior to all creation and possesses the highest status. All good qualities exist within Him in their most supreme form.

data.name: الْخَالِقُ
data.description: Al-Khaliq' means: He is the Being who can create anything without any prior model or example. He can create anything whenever He wills.

quran.data:
ہُوَ اللّٰہُ الۡخَالِقُ الۡبَارِئُ الۡمُصَوِّرُ لَہُ الۡاَسۡمَآءُ الۡحُسۡنٰی ؕ یُسَبِّحُ لَہٗ مَا فِی السَّمٰوٰتِ وَالۡاَرۡضِ ۚ وَہُوَ الۡعَزِیۡزُ الۡحَکِیۡمُ

He is Allah: the Creator, the Inventor, the Shaper. He ˹alone˺ has the Most Beautiful Names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth ˹constantly˺ glorifies Him. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

- Surah Al-Hashr, Verse: 24

data.name: الْبَارِئُ
data.description: Al-Bari' is the Being who grants existence and creates.

data.name: الْمُصَوِّرُ
data.description: Al-Musawwir' is the Being who adorns every creation with its own shape, form, and its own color and appearance.

Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْغَفَّارُ
data.description: Al-Ghaffar'—He is the Being who forgives repeatedly. Without exposing the servant's sins, He keeps them covered with a veil and forgives as many times as repentance is made. This name expresses the vastness of Allah's forgiveness and mercy.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْغَفَّارُ (Al-Ghaffar): The Repeatedly Forgiving. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْقَهَّارُ
data.description: Al-Qahhaar'—He is the Being who is All-Powerful, in whose hands lies the control of everything. No power can challenge Him. He holds power over all things.

quran.data:
یٰصَاحِبَیِ السِّجۡنِ ءَاَرۡبَابٌ مُّتَفَرِّقُوۡنَ خَیۡرٌ اَمِ اللّٰہُ الۡوَاحِدُ الۡقَہَّارُ

O my fellow-prisoners! Which is far better: many different lords or Allah—the One, the Supreme?

- Surah Yusuf, Verse: 39

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْقَهَّارُ (Al-Qahhar): The Subduer, before whom everyone is helpless and powerless. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْوَهَّابُ
data.description: Al-Wahhab'—He is the Being who is the Giver of everything and bestows upon everyone according to their capability.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْوَهَّابُ (Al-Wahhab): The Bestower of abundance without expecting anything in return. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الرَّزَّاقُ
data.description: Ar-Razzaq'—He is the one who provides sustenance and is also the creator of sustenance. And just as 'sustenance' (Rizq) refers to livelihood, it also refers to any kind of worldly or otherworldly blessing.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلرَّزَّاقُ (Ar-Razzaq): The Provider for all. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْفَتَّاحُ
data.description: Al-Fattah'—The Being who is the ultimate Decider. Whatever disagreements exist regarding truth and reality, Allah Ta'ala will provide the final resolution for everything.

quran.data:
قُلۡ یَجۡمَعُ بَیۡنَنَا رَبُّنَا ثُمَّ یَفۡتَحُ بَیۡنَنَا بِالۡحَقِّ ؕ وَہُوَ الۡفَتَّاحُ الۡعَلِیۡمُ

Say, “Our Lord will gather us together, then He will judge between us with the truth. For He is the All-Knowing Judge.”

- Surah Saba, Verse: 26

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful....اَلْفَتَّاحُ (Al-Fattah): The Opener of the doors of mercy and sustenance for all. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْعَلِيمُ
data.description: Al-Alim'—The Being whose knowledge is all-encompassing. There is no limit to His knowledge, nor is there any imperfection.

quran.data:
قُلۡ یَجۡمَعُ بَیۡنَنَا رَبُّنَا ثُمَّ یَفۡتَحُ بَیۡنَنَا بِالۡحَقِّ ؕ وَہُوَ الۡفَتَّاحُ الۡعَلِیۡمُ

Say, “Our Lord will gather us together, then He will judge between us with the truth. For He is the All-Knowing Judge.”

- Surah Saba, Verse: 26

اِنَّ رَبَّکَ ہُوَ الۡخَلّٰقُ الۡعَلِیۡمُ

Surely your Lord is the Master Creator, All-Knowing.

- Surah Al-Hijr, Verse: 86

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْعَلِیْمُ (Al-Alim): The All-Knowing of everything. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْقَابِضُ
data.description: Al-Qabid'—The Being who, according to His own will, sometimes restricts the life and livelihood of people. He decreases the sustenance and livelihood of whomever He wills, and increases it for whomever He wills. He decreases it whenever He wills, and increases it whenever He wills. He alone is the possessor of this authority, no one else.

hadith.data: "Indeed, Allah is the One Who fixes prices, the Withholder, the Expander, and the Provider. I hope to meet my Lord in a state where no one has a claim against me for an injustice regarding blood or property. Sunan Ibn Majah, Hadith No: 2200

data.name: الْبَاسِطُ
data.description: Al-Basit'—The One who expands. Just as the right to restrict belongs only to Him, the power to expand also belongs solely to Him. He can increase sustenance and all kinds of blessings, and can also decrease them if He wills.

hadith.data: "Indeed, Allah is the One Who fixes prices, the Withholder, the Expander, and the Provider. I hope to meet my Lord in a state where no one has a claim against me for an injustice regarding blood or property. Sunan Ibn Majah, Hadith No: 2200

data.name: الْخَافِضُ
data.description: Al-Khafid'—The Being who, according to His own will and wisdom, can humble or lower anyone in any aspect, such as life, livelihood, honor, fame, or status.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْخَافِضُ (Al-Khafid): The Abaser. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الرَّافِعُ
data.description: Ar-Rafi'—The Being who, according to His own will and wisdom, can elevate or exalt anyone in any aspect, such as life, livelihood, honor, fame, or status.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلرَّافِعُ (Ar-Rafi): The Exalter, meaning He alone raises or lowers whomever He wills, whenever He wills. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُعِزُّ
data.description: Al-Mu'izz'—The Being who, according to His own will and wisdom, can grant honor to anyone in any aspect, such as life, livelihood, respect, fame, or status.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْمُعِزُّ (Al-Mu'izz): The Giver of Honor. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُذِلُّ
data.description: Al-Mudhill'—The Being who, according to His own will and wisdom, can humiliate or degrade anyone in any aspect, such as life, livelihood, honor, fame, or status.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْمُذِلُّ (Al-Mudhill): The Giver of Dishonor; it is within His will to bestow honor or plunge someone into the depths of disgrace. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: السَّمِيعُ
data.description: As-Sami' means the Being who hears everything. Whatever words are spoken in the creation, whether openly or in secret, and no matter how high or low the sound, He hears everything.

quran.data:
وَلَہٗ مَا سَکَنَ فِی الَّیۡلِ وَالنَّہَارِ ؕ وَہُوَ السَّمِیۡعُ الۡعَلِیۡمُ

To Him belongs whatever exists in the day and night. And He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

- Surah Al-Anam, Verse: 13

وَلَا یَحۡزُنۡکَ قَوۡلُہُمۡ ۘ اِنَّ الۡعِزَّۃَ لِلّٰہِ جَمِیۡعًا ؕ ہُوَ السَّمِیۡعُ الۡعَلِیۡمُ

Do not let their words grieve you ˹O Prophet˺. Surely all honour and power belongs to Allah. He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

- Surah Yunus, Verse: 65

وَاِمَّا یَنۡزَغَنَّکَ مِنَ الشَّیۡطٰنِ نَزۡغٌ فَاسۡتَعِذۡ بِاللّٰہِ ؕ اِنَّہٗ ہُوَ السَّمِیۡعُ الۡعَلِیۡمُ

And if you are tempted by Satan, then seek refuge with Allah. Indeed, He ˹alone˺ is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

- Surah Fussilat, Verse: 36

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful....اَلسَّمِيْعُ (As-Sami): The All-Hearing. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْبَصِيرُ
data.description: Al-Basir' means the Being who sees everything. Nothing in the universe can remain hidden from His sight. He sees everything, whether hidden or manifest.

quran.data:
سُبۡحٰنَ الَّذِیۡۤ اَسۡرٰی بِعَبۡدِہٖ لَیۡلًا مِّنَ الۡمَسۡجِدِ الۡحَرَامِ اِلَی الۡمَسۡجِدِ الۡاَقۡصَا الَّذِیۡ بٰرَکۡنَا حَوۡلَہٗ لِنُرِیَہٗ مِنۡ اٰیٰتِنَا ؕ اِنَّہٗ ہُوَ السَّمِیۡعُ الۡبَصِیۡرُ

Glory be to the One Who took His servant ˹Muḥammad˺ by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque whose surroundings We have blessed, so that We may show him some of Our signs. Indeed, He alone is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

- Surah Al-Isra, Verse: 1

فَاطِرُ السَّمٰوٰتِ وَالۡاَرۡضِ ؕ جَعَلَ لَکُمۡ مِّنۡ اَنۡفُسِکُمۡ اَزۡوَاجًا وَّمِنَ الۡاَنۡعَامِ اَزۡوَاجًا ۚ یَذۡرَؤُکُمۡ فِیۡہِ ؕ لَیۡسَ کَمِثۡلِہٖ شَیۡءٌ ۚ وَہُوَ السَّمِیۡعُ الۡبَصِیۡرُ

˹He is˺ the Originator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you spouses from among yourselves, and ˹made˺ mates for cattle ˹as well˺—multiplying you ˹both˺. There is nothing like Him, for He ˹alone˺ is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

- Surah Ash-Shura, Verse: 11

data.name: الْحَكَمُ
data.description: Al-Hakam' means He is the ultimate Judge. All disagreements regarding truth and reality will be settled by Him in the afterlife. The rights of Allah and the rights of the servants—everything will be presented in His court. He alone will give the verdict.

data.name: الْعَدْلُ
data.description: Al-Adl' means Allah Ta'ala is Most Just. On the Day of Resurrection, He will judge between His servants based on extreme fairness and justice. Just as the reward for every small and large good deed of this world will be given on that day, humans will also find every small and large injustice, oppression, and sin present before them. Allah Ta'ala will decide these matters according to His own justice.

data.name: اللَّطِيفُ
data.description: Al-Latif'—A Being whom human senses cannot grasp. He is the Being who is aware of even the most subtle aspects of all matters, and He is exceedingly gentle in guiding His servants.

quran.data:
لَا تُدۡرِکُہُ الۡاَبۡصَارُ ۫ وَہُوَ یُدۡرِکُ الۡاَبۡصَارَ ۚ وَہُوَ اللَّطِیۡفُ الۡخَبِیۡرُ

No vision can encompass Him, but He encompasses all vision. For He is the Most Subtle, All-Aware.

- Surah Al-Anam, Verse: 103

اَلَا یَعۡلَمُ مَنۡ خَلَقَ ؕ وَہُوَ اللَّطِیۡفُ الۡخَبِیۡرُ

How could He not know His Own creation? For He ˹alone˺ is the Most Subtle, All-Aware.

- Surah Al-Mulk, Verse: 14

Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْخَبِيرُ
data.description: Al-Khabir'—The Being who keeps track of everything. He is well-acquainted with all matters.

quran.data:
وَہُوَ الۡقَاہِرُ فَوۡقَ عِبَادِہٖ ؕ وَہُوَ الۡحَکِیۡمُ الۡخَبِیۡرُ

He reigns supreme over His creation. And He is the All-Wise, All-Aware.

- Surah Al-Anam, Verse: 18

لَا تُدۡرِکُہُ الۡاَبۡصَارُ ۫ وَہُوَ یُدۡرِکُ الۡاَبۡصَارَ ۚ وَہُوَ اللَّطِیۡفُ الۡخَبِیۡرُ

data.name: الْحَلِيمُ
data.description: Al-Halim' means He is Most Forbearing. When a servant commits a wrong, He does not punish them immediately. He gives them respite. He gives them the opportunity to return.

quran.data:
قَالُوۡا یٰشُعَیۡبُ اَصَلٰوتُکَ تَاۡمُرُکَ اَنۡ نَّتۡرُکَ مَا یَعۡبُدُ اٰبَآؤُنَاۤ اَوۡ اَنۡ نَّفۡعَلَ فِیۡۤ اَمۡوَالِنَا مَا نَشٰٓؤُا ؕ اِنَّکَ لَاَنۡتَ الۡحَلِیۡمُ الرَّشِیۡدُ

They asked ˹sarcastically˺, “O Shu’aib! Does your prayer command you that we should abandon what our forefathers worshipped or give up managing our wealth as we please? Indeed, you are such a tolerant, sensible man!”

- Surah Hud, Verse: 87

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. data.name: الْعَظِيمُ
data.description: Al-Azim' means He is Magnificent. He is Great in both His essence and His attributes. There is no comparison to His magnificence. No one can be equal to Him.

quran.data:
فَسَبِّحۡ بِاسۡمِ رَبِّکَ الۡعَظِیۡمِ

So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest.

- Surah Al-Haqqah, Verse: 52

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful. ... اَلْعَظِيْمُ (Al-Azim): The Magnificent, the Most High. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْغَفُورُ
data.description: Al-Ghafur' means He is the All-Forgiving. However great or numerous a person's sins may be, His attribute of forgiveness is far greater. He loves to forgive.

quran.data:
قَالَ سَوۡفَ اَسۡتَغۡفِرُ لَکُمۡ رَبِّیۡ ؕ اِنَّہٗ ہُوَ الۡغَفُوۡرُ الرَّحِیۡمُ

He said, “I will pray to my Lord for your forgiveness. He ˹alone˺ is indeed the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.”

- Surah Yusuf, Verse: 98

نَبِّیٴۡ عِبَادِیۡۤ اَنِّیۡۤ اَنَا الۡغَفُوۡرُ الرَّحِیۡمُ

Inform My servants ˹O Prophet˺ that I am truly the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful,

- Surah Al-Hijr, Verse: 49

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الشَّكُورُ
data.description: Ash-Shakur' means He is the Appreciative. He will grant the appropriate reward for any good deed of the servant. Based on sincerity and intention, the scale of that reward will increase from 10 times to 700 times, and even more than that.

quran.data:
قَالَ سَوۡفَ اَسۡتَغۡفِرُ لَکُمۡ رَبِّیۡ ؕ اِنَّہٗ ہُوَ الۡغَفُوۡرُ الرَّحِیۡمُ

Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْعَلِيُّ
data.description: Al-Aliy' means He is the Most High. No one can be equal to Him in status and majesty. No one can even come close.

quran.data:
وَسِعَ کُرۡسِیُّہُ السَّمٰوٰتِ وَالۡاَرۡضَ ۚ وَلَا یَـُٔوۡدُہٗ حِفۡظُہُمَا ۚ وَہُوَ الۡعَلِیُّ الۡعَظِیۡمُ

Allah! There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him, the Ever-Living, All-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness nor sleep overtakes Him. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who could possibly intercede with Him without His permission? He ˹fully˺ knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them, but no one can grasp any of His knowledge—except what He wills ˹to reveal˺. His Seat encompasses the heavens and the earth, and the preservation of both does not tire Him. For He is the Most High, the Greatest.

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 255

اِنَّ اللّٰہَ کَانَ عَلِیًّا کَبِیۡرًا

Men are the caretakers of women, as men have been provisioned by Allah over women and tasked with supporting them financially. And righteous women are devoutly obedient and, when alone, protective of what Allah has entrusted them with. And if you sense ill-conduct from your women, advise them ˹first˺, ˹if they persist,˺ do not share their beds, ˹but if they still persist,˺ then discipline them ˹gently˺. But if they change their ways, do not be unjust to them. Surely Allah is Most High, All-Great.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 34

وَاَنَّ اللّٰہَ ہُوَ الۡعَلِیُّ الۡکَبِیۡرُ

That is because Allah ˹alone˺ is the Truth and what they invoke besides Him is falsehood, and ˹because˺ Allah ˹alone˺ is the Most High, All-Great.

- Surah Luqman, Verse: 30

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْحَفِيظُ
data.description: Al-Hafiz' means He is the All-Protector. He is preserving the entire universe. It is He who protects us from all kinds of dangers.

quran.data:
وَرَبُّکَ عَلٰی کُلِّ شَیۡءٍ حَفِیۡظٌ 

He does not have any authority over them, but ˹Our Will is˺ only to distinguish those who believe in the Hereafter from those who are in doubt about it. And your Lord is a ˹vigilant˺ Keeper over all things.

- Surah Saba, Verse: 21

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْحَفِيْظُ (Al-Hafiz): The Preserver of all. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُقِيتُ
data.description: Al-Muqit' means the One who is All-Powerful, the Preserver, and the Witness of everything. It is He who provides sustenance to everyone.

quran.data:
وَکَانَ اللّٰہُ عَلٰی کُلِّ شَیۡءٍ مُّقِیۡتًا

Whoever intercedes for a good cause will have a share in the reward, and whoever intercedes for an evil cause will have a share in the burden. And Allah is Watchful over all things.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 85

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْمُقِيْتُ (Al-Muqit): The Nourisher who supplies the means of life to all. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْحَسِيبُ
data.description: Al-Hasib' means He will take account. He alone will take the account of all the deeds of the servants. That accounting of His will be extremely just. Not even the slightest injustice will be done to anyone.

quran.data:
وَکَفٰی بِاللّٰہِ حَسِیۡبًا

Test ˹the competence of˺ the orphans until they reach a marriageable age. Then if you feel they are capable of sound judgment, return their wealth to them. And do not consume it wastefully and hastily before they grow up ˹to demand it˺. If the guardian is well-off, they should not take compensation; but if the guardian is poor, let them take a reasonable provision. When you give orphans back their property, call in witnesses. And sufficient is Allah as a ˹vigilant˺ Reckoner.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 6

اِنَّ اللّٰہَ کَانَ عَلٰی کُلِّ شَیۡءٍ حَسِیۡبًا

And when you are greeted, respond with a better greeting or at least similarly. Surely Allah is a ˹vigilant˺ Reckoner of all things.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 86

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْحَسِيْبُ (Al-Hasib): The Sufficient One for all. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْجَلِيلُ
data.description: Al-Jalil' means He is the Possessor of infinite majesty. No one can fully comprehend or encompass Him. All creations that are great and magnificent were created by Him.

quran.data:
وَّیَبۡقٰی وَجۡہُ رَبِّکَ ذُو الۡجَلٰلِ وَالۡاِکۡرَامِ

Only your Lord Himself, full of Majesty and Honour, will remain ˹forever˺.

- Surah Ar-Rahman, Verse: 27

تَبٰرَکَ اسۡمُ رَبِّکَ ذِی الۡجَلٰلِ وَالۡاِکۡرَامِ

Blessed is the Name of your Lord, full of Majesty and Honour.

- Surah Ar-Rahman, Verse: 78

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful....اَلْجَلِيْلُ (Al-Jalil): The Majestic. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْكَرِيمُ
data.description: Al-Karim' means He is the Most Honorable. The Most Generous. All honor is due to Him.

quran.data:
یٰۤاَیُّہَا الۡاِنۡسَانُ مَا غَرَّکَ بِرَبِّکَ الۡکَرِیۡمِ

O humanity! What has emboldened you against your Lord, the Most Generous,

- Surah Al-Infitar, Verse: 6

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful....اَلْكَرِيْمُ (Al-Karim): The Most Generous. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الرَّقِيبُ
data.description: Ar-Raqib' means He oversees everything. He keeps a watchful eye over all things. Nothing can remain outside of His sight and supervision.

quran.data:
اِنَّ اللّٰہَ کَانَ عَلَیۡکُمۡ رَقِیۡبًا

O humanity! Be mindful of your Lord Who created you from a single soul, and from it He created its mate, and through both He spread countless men and women. And be mindful of Allah—in Whose Name you appeal to one another—and ˹honour˺ family ties. Surely Allah is ever Watchful over you.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 1

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلرَّقِيْبُ (Ar-Raqib): The Watchful and Guardian. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُجِيبُ
data.description: Al-Mujib' means the One who responds to the call of the servants. However a servant calls upon Allah, Allah hears it and He responds to that call. He alone can fulfill the needs of the servants.

quran.data:
اِنَّ رَبِّیۡ قَرِیۡبٌ مُّجِیۡبٌ

And to the people of Thamûd We sent their brother Ṣâliḥ. He said, “O my people! Worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. He ˹is the One Who˺ produced you from the earth and settled you on it. So seek His forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance. Surely my Lord is Ever Near, All-Responsive ˹to prayers˺.”

- Surah Hud, Verse: 61

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْمُجِيْبُ (Al-Mujib): The Responsive. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْوَاسِعُ
data.description: Al-Wasi' means He is the All-Embracing. This vastness is in every aspect—His power and authority, knowledge, mercy and compassion, grace—everything is all-encompassing.

quran.data:
وَاللّٰہُ یُضٰعِفُ لِمَنۡ یَّشَآءُ ؕ وَاللّٰہُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِیۡمٌ

The example of those who spend their wealth in the cause of Allah is that of a grain that sprouts into seven ears, each bearing one hundred grains. And Allah multiplies ˹the reward even more˺ to whoever He wills. For Allah is All-Bountiful, All-Knowing.

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 261

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. data.name: الْحَكِيمُ
data.description: Al-Hakim' means He is the All-Wise. He is the Owner of perfect wisdom and prudence. None of His actions are devoid of wisdom.

quran.data:
قَالُوۡا سُبۡحٰنَکَ لَا عِلۡمَ لَنَاۤ اِلَّا مَا عَلَّمۡتَنَا ؕ اِنَّکَ اَنۡتَ الۡعَلِیۡمُ الۡحَکِیۡمُ

They replied, “Glory be to You! We have no knowledge except what You have taught us. You are truly the All-Knowing, All-Wise.”

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 32

یُرِیۡدُ اللّٰہُ لِیُبَیِّنَ لَکُمۡ وَیَہۡدِیَکُمۡ سُنَنَ الَّذِیۡنَ مِنۡ قَبۡلِکُمۡ وَیَتُوۡبَ عَلَیۡکُمۡ ؕ وَاللّٰہُ عَلِیۡمٌ حَکِیۡمٌ

It is Allah’s Will to make things clear to you, guide you to the ˹noble˺ ways of those before you, and turn to you in mercy. For Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 26

قَدۡ فَرَضَ اللّٰہُ لَکُمۡ تَحِلَّۃَ اَیۡمَانِکُمۡ ۚ وَاللّٰہُ مَوۡلٰىکُمۡ ۚ وَہُوَ الۡعَلِیۡمُ الۡحَکِیۡمُ

Allah has already ordained for you ˹believers˺ the way to absolve yourselves from your oaths. For Allah is your Guardian. And He is the All-Knowing, All-Wise.

- Surah At-Tahrim, Verse: 2

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْوَدُودُ
data.description: Al-Wadud' means the Loving One. He loves His servants very much. His infinite benevolence and mercy towards His servants are the expressions of His love.

quran.data:
وَاسۡتَغۡفِرُوۡا رَبَّکُمۡ ثُمَّ تُوۡبُوۡۤا اِلَیۡہِ ؕ اِنَّ رَبِّیۡ رَحِیۡمٌ وَّدُوۡدٌ

So seek your Lord’s forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance. Surely my Lord is Most Merciful, All-Loving.”

- Surah Hud, Verse: 90

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْمَجِيدُ
data.description: Al-Majid' means He is the Most Glorious. No one can be equal to Him in majesty, nor can anyone even come close.

quran.data:
قَالُوۡۤا اَتَعۡجَبِیۡنَ مِنۡ اَمۡرِ اللّٰہِ رَحۡمَتُ اللّٰہِ وَبَرَکٰتُہٗ عَلَیۡکُمۡ اَہۡلَ الۡبَیۡتِ ؕ اِنَّہٗ حَمِیۡدٌ مَّجِیۡدٌ

They responded, “Are you astonished by Allah’s decree? May Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you, O people of this house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy, All-Glorious.”

- Surah Hud, Verse: 73

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful....اَلْمَجِيْدُ (Al-Majid): The All-Glorious. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْبَاعِثُ
data.description: Al-Ba'ith' means He will resurrect us in the hereafter. There, the account of all the good and bad deeds of this world will be taken.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الشَّهِيدُ
data.description: Ash-Shahid' means He is witnessing everything. As an Observer and Witness, He alone is sufficient.

quran.data:
وَکَفٰی بِاللّٰہِ شَہِیۡدًا

Yet ˹if you are denied, O Prophet,˺ Allah bears witness to what He has sent down to you—He has sent it with His knowledge. The angels too bear witness. And Allah ˹alone˺ is sufficient as a Witness.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 166

اِنَّ اللّٰہَ عَلٰی کُلِّ شَیۡءٍ شَہِیۡدٌ

Indeed, the believers, Jews, Sabians, Christians, Magi, and the polytheists—Allah will judge between them ˹all˺ on Judgment Day. Surely Allah is a Witness over all things.

- Surah Al-Hajj, Verse: 17

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْحَقُّ
data.description: Al-Haqq' means He is the Eternal Truth. He has created everything in accordance with His wisdom.

quran.data:
ذٰلِکَ بِاَنَّ اللّٰہَ ہُوَ الۡحَقُّ وَاَنَّہٗ یُحۡیِ الۡمَوۡتٰی وَاَنَّہٗ عَلٰی کُلِّ شَیۡءٍ قَدِیۡرٌ

That is because Allah ˹alone˺ is the Truth, He ˹alone˺ gives life to the dead, and He ˹alone˺ is Most Capable of everything.

- Surah Al-Hajj, Verse: 6

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْوَكِيلُ
data.description: Al-Wakil' means He is the Disposer of affairs. Therefore, trust can only be placed in Him and no one else.

quran.data:

Those who were warned, “Your enemies have mobilized their forces against you, so fear them,” the warning only made them grow stronger in faith and they replied, “Allah ˹alone˺ is sufficient ˹as an aid˺ for us and ˹He˺ is the best Protector.”

- Surah Ali-Imran, Verse: 173

وَکَفٰی بِاللّٰہِ وَکِیۡلًا

And they say, “We obey,” but when they leave you, a group of them would spend the night contradicting what they said. Allah records all their schemes. So turn away from them, and put your trust in Allah. And Allah is sufficient as a Trustee of Affairs.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 81

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْقَوِيُّ
data.description: Al-Qawi' means He is the Possessor of great strength. There is no one to challenge His power. No one has the strength to go against His will.

quran.data:
اِنَّ اللّٰہَ لَقَوِیٌّ عَزِیۡزٌ

˹They are˺ those who have been expelled from their homes for no reason other than proclaiming: “Our Lord is Allah.” Had Allah not repelled ˹the aggression of˺ some people by means of others, destruction would have surely claimed monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques in which Allah’s Name is often mentioned. Allah will certainly help those who stand up for Him. Allah is truly All-Powerful, Almighty.

- Surah Al-Hajj, Verse: 40

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْمَتِينُ
data.description: Al-Matin' means He is the Possessor of great power, the All-Powerful.

data.name: الْوَلِيُّ
data.description: Al-Wali' means the Protector/Guardian. He is the Guardian of the believers. He guides the believers to the path of faith.

quran.data:
وَہُوَ الَّذِیۡ یُنَزِّلُ الۡغَیۡثَ مِنۡۢ بَعۡدِ مَا قَنَطُوۡا وَیَنۡشُرُ رَحۡمَتَہٗ ؕ وَہُوَ الۡوَلِیُّ الۡحَمِیۡدُ

He is the One Who sends down rain after people have given up hope, spreading out His mercy. He is the Guardian, the Praiseworthy.

- Surah Ash-Shura, Verse: 28

data.description: Al-Hamid' means the One worthy of praise. He alone is the sole possessor of all the praises in the universe. Whatever abilities humans or the creation possess are also His gifts. Therefore, He is the only entity worthy of receiving all praise.

- Surah Ash-Shura, Verse: 28

قَالُوۡۤا اَتَعۡجَبِیۡنَ مِنۡ اَمۡرِ اللّٰہِ رَحۡمَتُ اللّٰہِ وَبَرَکٰتُہٗ عَلَیۡکُمۡ اَہۡلَ الۡبَیۡتِ ؕ اِنَّہٗ حَمِیۡدٌ مَّجِیۡدٌ

They responded, “Are you astonished by Allah’s decree? May Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you, O people of this house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy, All-Glorious.”

It cannot be proven false from any angle. ˹It is˺ a revelation from the ˹One Who is˺ All-Wise, Praiseworthy.

- Surah Fussilat, Verse: 42

data.name: الْمُحْصِي
data.description: Al-Muhsi' means the One who keeps account. The One who tallies. He keeps account of all the deeds of the servants, whether they are small or large.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْمُحْصِيُ (Al-Muhsi): The Counter, who is fully aware and knowledgeable of all creation. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُبْدِئُ
data.description: Al-Mubdi' means He originated this universe for the first time. He created this world without any prior model.

quran.data:
اِنَّہٗ ہُوَ یُبۡدِئُ وَیُعِیۡدُ

˹For˺ He is certainly the One Who originates and resurrects ˹all˺.

- Surah Al-Buruj, Verse: 13

Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُعِيدُ
data.description: Al-Mu'id' means Allah the Almighty will destroy this world once. Then He will recreate it. For the One who can create the first time without a model, creating it a second time is no matter at all.

quran.data:
اِنَّہٗ ہُوَ یُبۡدِئُ وَیُعِیۡدُ

˹For˺ He is certainly the One Who originates and resurrects ˹all˺.

- Surah Al-Buruj, Verse: 13

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful....اَلْمُعِيِدُ (Al-Mu'id): The Restorer of life. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُحْيِي
data.description: Al-Muhyi' means the Giver of Life. He is the only entity who can grant life. Just as He sends someone to the world by giving life, in the same way, He also preserves life. Someone can remain in this world only as long as He wills.

quran.data:
ہُوَ یُحۡیٖ وَیُمِیۡتُ وَاِلَیۡہِ تُرۡجَعُوۡنَ

He ˹is the One Who˺ gives life and causes death, and to Him you will ˹all˺ be returned.

- Surah Yunus, Verse: 56

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْمُحْيِي (Al-Muhyi): The Giver of Life. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُمِيتُ
data.description: Al-Mumit' means the Bringer of Death. Death occurs to someone only when He wills it. Just as no one can receive life against His will, no one can die against His will. Death will occur only when He grants it.

quran.data:
ہُوَ یُحۡیٖ وَیُمِیۡتُ وَاِلَیۡہِ تُرۡجَعُوۡنَ

He ˹is the One Who˺ gives life and causes death, and to Him you will ˹all˺ be returned.

- Surah Yunus, Verse: 56

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلْمُمِيْتُ (Al-Mumit): The Giver of Death. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْحَيُّ
data.description: Al-Hayy' means He is the Ever-Living. When nothing of the creation existed, He was there. Again, a time will come when He will destroy everything. Even then, only He will remain.

quran.data:
اَللّٰہُ لَاۤ اِلٰہَ اِلَّا ہُوَ ۚ اَلۡحَیُّ الۡقَیُّوۡمُ

Allah! There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him, the Ever-Living, All-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness nor sleep overtakes Him. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who could possibly intercede with Him without His permission? He ˹fully˺ knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them, but no one can grasp any of His knowledge—except what He wills ˹to reveal˺. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who could possibly intercede with Him without His permission? He ˹fully˺ knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them, but no one can grasp any of His knowledge—except what He wills ˹to reveal˺. - Surah Yusuf, Verse: 39

- Surah Al-Ikhlas, Verse: 1

Allah—the Sustainer ˹needed by all˺.

- Surah Al-Ikhlas, Verse: 2

data.name: الْقَادِرُ
data.description: Al-Qadir' means the Powerful. His power is incomparable. Nothing in the universe is outside the scope of His power.

quran.data:

- Surah Al-Anam, Verse: 65

- Surah Al-Qamar, Verse: 42

hadith.data: "You are the Advancer, and You are the Delayer; there is no god but You, and there is no god besides You, and there is no power and no strength except with You. Sunan Ibn Majah, Hadith No: 1355

data.name: التَّوَّابُ
data.description: It means He accepts the repentance of His servant for any offense. No matter how great the wrongdoing is, when someone repents with a sincere heart, Allah accepts it.

quran.data:
رَبَّنَا وَاجۡعَلۡنَا مُسۡلِمَیۡنِ لَکَ وَمِنۡ ذُرِّیَّتِنَاۤ اُمَّۃً مُّسۡلِمَۃً لَّکَ ۪ وَاَرِنَا مَنَاسِکَنَا وَتُبۡ عَلَیۡنَا ۚ اِنَّکَ اَنۡتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِیۡمُ

Our Lord! Make us both ˹fully˺ submit to You and from our descendants a nation that will submit to You. Show us our rituals, and turn to us in grace. You are truly the Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful.

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 128

اِلَّا الَّذِیۡنَ تَابُوۡا وَاَصۡلَحُوۡا وَبَیَّنُوۡا فَاُولٰٓئِکَ اَتُوۡبُ عَلَیۡہِمۡ ۚ وَاَنَا التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِیۡمُ

As for those who repent, mend their ways, and let the truth be known, they are the ones to whom I will turn ˹in forgiveness˺, for I am the Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful.

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 160

data.name: الْمُنْتَقِمُ
data.description: He will punish the servant for their sins. Giving punishment is as if taking retribution. He can forgive any offense of His servants if He wills, and He can also punish for any offense if He wills.

quran.data:

And who does more wrong than the one who is reminded of Allah’s revelations then turns away from them? We will surely inflict punishment upon the wicked.

- Surah As-Sajdah, Verse: 22

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْعَفُوُّ
data.description: Al-Afuww' means He is the Most Forgiving. No matter how great or how many a person's sins are, His attribute of forgiveness is much greater. He loves to forgive.

quran.data:
اِنَّ اللّٰہَ کَانَ عَفُوًّا غَفُوۡرًا

O believers! Do not approach prayer while intoxicated until you are aware of what you say, nor in a state of ˹full˺ impurity—unless you merely pass through ˹the mosque˺—until you have bathed. But if you are ill, on a journey, or have relieved yourselves, or been intimate with your wives and cannot find water, then purify yourselves with clean earth, wiping your faces and hands. And Allah is Ever-Pardoning, All-Forgiving.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 43

اِنۡ تُبۡدُوۡا خَیۡرًا اَوۡ تُخۡفُوۡہُ اَوۡ تَعۡفُوۡا عَنۡ سُوۡٓءٍ فَاِنَّ اللّٰہَ کَانَ عَفُوًّا قَدِیۡرًا

Whether you reveal or conceal a good or pardon an evil—surely Allah is Ever-Pardoning, Most Capable.

- Surah An-Nisa, Verse: 149

And there are those who would dedicate their lives to Allah’s pleasure. And Allah is Ever Gracious to ˹His˺ servants.

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 207

اِنَّہٗ بِہِمۡ رَءُوۡفٌ رَّحِیۡمٌ

Allah has certainly turned in mercy to the Prophet as well as the Emigrants and the Helpers who stood by him in the time of hardship, after the hearts of a group of them had almost faltered. He then accepted their repentance. Surely He is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to them.

- Surah At-Tawbah, Verse: 117

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... اَلرَّؤُوْفُ (Ar-Ra'uf): The Most Kind. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: مَالِكُ الْمُلْكِ
data.description: He is the Owner of all sovereignty. All kings, all rulers, and all those in power are under His sole authority.

quran.data:
قُلِ اللّٰہُمَّ مٰلِکَ الۡمُلۡکِ تُؤۡتِی الۡمُلۡکَ مَنۡ تَشَآءُ وَتَنۡزِعُ الۡمُلۡکَ مِمَّنۡ تَشَآءُ ۫ وَتُعِزُّ مَنۡ تَشَآءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَنۡ تَشَآءُ ؕ بِیَدِکَ الۡخَیۡرُ ؕ اِنَّکَ عَلٰی کُلِّ شَیۡءٍ قَدِیۡرٌ

Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “O Allah! Lord over all authorities! You give authority to whoever You please and remove it from who You please; You honour whoever You please and disgrace who You please—all good is in Your Hands. Surely You ˹alone˺ are Most Capable of everything.

- Surah Ali-Imran, Verse: 26

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: ذُو الْجَلاَلِ وَالإِكْرَامِ
data.translation: The Lord of Majesty and Generosity
data.description: He is the possessor of honor and dignity. In terms of honor, excellence, dignity, and greatness, no one is His equal, nor is anyone even close.

quran.data:
وَّیَبۡقٰی وَجۡہُ رَبِّکَ ذُو الۡجَلٰلِ وَالۡاِکۡرَامِ

Only your Lord Himself, full of Majesty and Honour, will remain ˹forever˺.

- Surah Ar-Rahman, Verse: 27

تَبٰرَکَ اسۡمُ رَبِّکَ ذِی الۡجَلٰلِ وَالۡاِکۡرَامِ

Blessed is the Name of your Lord, full of Majesty and Honour.

- Surah Ar-Rahman, Verse: 78

Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمُقْسِطُ
data.description: In the afterlife, He will judge His servants with equity and justice. He will not do injustice to anyone. He will not punish anyone more than their sins, nor will He diminish anyone's rewards.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْجَامِعُ
data.description: On the Day of Judgment, He will gather all people together. It is possible only for Him to gather everyone who has come and is yet to come into this world.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْغَنِيُّ
data.description: Al-Ghani' means He is free of want. There is not even a trace of need in Him. Rather, He is abundant in every aspect. There is no lack of anything with Him.

quran.data:
وَاللّٰہُ غَنِیٌّ حَلِیۡمٌ

Kind words and forgiveness are better than charity followed by injury. And Allah is Self-Sufficient, Most Forbearing.

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 263

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْمُغْنِي
data.description: He alleviates the needs of His servants. He makes the servant wealthy. The power to bestow wealth is in His hands alone.

quran.data:
وَاَنَّہٗ ہُوَ اَغۡنٰی وَاَقۡنٰی

And He is the One Who enriches and impoverishes.

- Surah An-Najm, Verse: 48

Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الْمَانِعُ
data.description: He protects His servants from the hands of the enemy, and He alone helps against the enemy. Again, if He wills, He keeps someone away from His blessings.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الضَّارُّ
data.translation: The Inflictor of Harm (at His will)
data.description: When He wishes to test someone with any harm, He does so. He makes whom He wills ill, He makes whom He wills poor, and again, when He wills, He removes this poverty and illness.

data.name: النَّافِعُ
data.description: Benefiting the servant is also within His control. If He wills, He can benefit anyone in any way. He alone is the owner of benefit and harm, no one else.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: النُّورُ
data.description: Allah Ta'ala is 'An-Nur', meaning all creations of the world receive the light of guidance from Allah Ta'ala alone.

quran.data:
اَللّٰہُ نُوۡرُ السَّمٰوٰتِ وَالۡاَرۡضِ

Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. His light is like a niche in which there is a lamp, the lamp is in a crystal, the crystal is like a shining star, lit from ˹the oil of˺ a blessed olive tree, ˹located˺ neither to the east nor the west, whose oil would almost glow, even without being touched by fire. Light upon light! Allah guides whoever He wills to His light. And Allah sets forth parables for humanity. For Allah has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of all things.

- Surah An-Nur, Verse: 35

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْهَادِي
data.description: He shows the right path and guides along it. If He does not guide someone to the right path, then everyone in the world together will not be able to give them guidance.

quran.data:
وَاِنَّ اللّٰہَ لَہَادِ الَّذِیۡنَ اٰمَنُوۡۤا اِلٰی صِرَاطٍ مُّسۡتَقِیۡمٍ

˹This is˺ also so that those gifted with knowledge would know that this ˹revelation˺ is the truth from your Lord, so they have faith in it, and so their hearts would submit humbly to it. And Allah surely guides the believers to the Straight Path.

- Surah Al-Hajj, Verse: 54

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. quran.data:
بَدِیۡعُ السَّمٰوٰتِ وَالۡاَرۡضِ

˹He is˺ the Originator of the heavens and the earth! When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, “Be!” And it is!

- Surah Al-Baqarah, Verse: 117

data.name: الْبَاقِي
data.description: He is Everlasting, Ever-Living. At a time when everything will be destroyed, He will still remain, in His own glory and majesty.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. data.name: الْوَارِثُ
data.description: The ultimate inheritance of the world belongs to Him. Everyone will perish; no one will remain. Only He will remain. Therefore, no one besides Him can be the sole ultimate heir of the heavens and the earth.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الرَّشِيدُ
data.translation: The Guide to the Right Path
data.description: He shows His servants the right path and points out the way to wellbeing in both religion and the world.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30

data.name: الصَّبُورُ
data.description: He is Most Patient. When a servant indulges in sin, He does not punish immediately; He gives them the opportunity to repent, to return. It is unimaginable how much audacity it is to disobey His commands while enjoying the blessings given by Him! Yet, even after this, He continues to give opportunities to His servants.

hadith.data: Narrated by Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah Ta'ala has one less than a hundred, i.e., ninety-nine names. Whoever memorizes them and keeps them in mind will enter Paradise." (The details of those holy names are as follows) He is Allah, besides whom there is no other deity or anyone worthy of worship. He is— اَلرَّحْمٰنُ (Ar-Rahman): The Most Compassionate. اَلرَّحِيْمُ (Ar-Rahim): The Most Merciful.... الصَّبُورُ (As-Sabur): The Most Patient, who does not immediately punish despite witnessing extreme audacity and disobedience. Ma'ariful Hadith, Hadith: 30
